skill.name: gws-sheets-read
skill.description: Read values from a Google Sheets spreadsheet using the gws CLI tool.
skill.summary: Read data from Google Sheets with range selection and spreadsheet targeting.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: All 12 static findings are false positives. The backtick detection triggered on markdown code fences (```bash), path traversal on relative markdown links (../gws-shared/), and weak crypto warnings on YAML flag names containing 'crypt'. This is a read-only Google Sheets access tool with no external command injection, filesystem vulnerabilities, or cryptographic risks.

content.user_title: Read Google Sheets data with gws CLI
content.value_statement: Users need to extract data from Google Sheets without manual copy-paste. This skill provides CLI-based spreadsheet reading with range selection for precise data extraction.
actual_capabilities: Read cell values from specific ranges in Google Sheets (e.g. 'Sheet1!A1:B2')
Read entire sheet tabs by specifying the sheet name
Target specific spreadsheets by ID for precise data access
Output structured data for use in other Claude or Codex workflows
Integrate Google Sheets data into automation pipelines
limitations: Read-only operation - cannot write, update, or delete spreadsheet data
Requires authenticated gws CLI setup with valid Google credentials
Limited to ranges supported by Google Sheets API (cannot read formulas as computed values)

use_cases.title: Export spreadsheet data for analysis
use_cases.description: Extract rows from a pricing sheet to compare against competitors or generate reports.

use_cases.title: Pull metrics into documentation
use_cases.description: Read KPI values from a tracking sheet and incorporate them into generated status reports.

use_cases.title: Feed sheet data to automation workflows
use_cases.description: Retrieve configuration or lookup table values from Sheets to use in CI/CD pipelines.

prompt_templates.title: Basic sheet read
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the data from spreadsheet [SPREADSHEET_ID] in range Sheet1!A1:D10
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to quickly pull a table from Google Sheets

prompt_templates.title: Read specific tab
prompt_templates.prompt: Get all data from the 'Metrics' tab in spreadsheet [SPREADSHEET_ID]
prompt_templates.scenario: When you know the sheet name but want the full tab contents

prompt_templates.title: Extract single value
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the value at cell B5 on Sheet1 in spreadsheet [SPREADSHEET_ID]
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a specific cell value for validation or comparison

prompt_templates.title: Combine with analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Read range A1:F50 from the 'Q4_Data' tab in [SPREADSHEET_ID] and summarize the key trends
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to read data and have Claude analyze or summarize it

output_examples.input: Read range A1:C3 from spreadsheet 1B2C3D4E5F6G7H8 in Sheet1

best_practices: Always verify spreadsheet ID is correct before running the command to avoid fetching wrong data
Use specific ranges instead of full sheet reads to reduce data transfer and improve performance
Store spreadsheet IDs in environment variables or configuration files rather than hardcoding in prompts
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to read sensitive data without proper access controls - ensure Google account has read-only permissions
Avoid reading very large ranges (thousands of rows) in a single call - paginate or filter ranges instead
Do not attempt to modify spreadsheet data with this skill - it is strictly read-only

faq.question: What permissions do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a Google account with at least read access to the spreadsheet. The gws CLI must be authenticated with Google OAuth.

faq.question: Can I read formulas or only values?
faq.answer: This skill reads cell values as they appear. To get computed formula results, ensure the spreadsheet has been recalculated or use the values.get API directly.

faq.question: How do I find my spreadsheet ID?
faq.answer: Open the spreadsheet in Google Sheets. The ID appears in the URL between '/d/' and '/edit'. Example: docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/SPREADSHEET_ID_HERE/edit

faq.question: Can I filter rows while reading?
faq.answer: This skill reads the exact range you specify. Use A1 notation to define your range precisely. Complex filtering requires additional processing after reading.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use with confidential spreadsheets?
faq.answer: Yes - this is a read-only operation that does not modify or export data externally. Data stays within your authenticated Google session.

faq.question: Why am I getting authentication errors?
faq.answer: Run 'gws auth login' to authenticate with Google. Ensure your credentials are still valid and the spreadsheet is shared with your Google account.
